items.title: Hungarians vote in big numbers on whether to end Orbán rule and elect rival
items.summary: Most polls favour Péter Magyar, who fronts a grassroots party, and early figures point to a record turnout of voters.

items.title: 'We need real peace': Easter truce fails to lift grim mood in war-torn Ukraine
items.summary: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

items.title: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
items.summary: Her infectious voice got fans dancing and singing, becoming the soundtrack for generations of Indians.

items.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
items.summary: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

items.title: Prosecutor probes abuse claims against California governor candidate Swalwell
items.summary: The high-profile Democrat, a leading contender in California's governor race, firmly denies the accusations.

items.title: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
items.summary: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

items.title: Germany's far-right AfD adopts 'radical' manifesto ahead of key polls
items.summary: The far-right party is riding high in the opinion polls in the eastern state of Saxony-Anhalt.

items.title: Knifeman calling himself 'Lucifer' slashes three at NYC's Grand Central
items.summary: Police fatally shot the assailant after he ignored at least 20 demands to drop the weapon, NYPD says.

items.title: Coachella kicks off with Sabrina Carpenter and surprise guests
items.summary: The festival in California was also forced to cancel a set by DJ Anyma on Friday because of strong winds.

items.title: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
items.summary: Judges handed down sentences ranging from five years to life imprisonment following mass trials.

items.title: Artemis crew home safely after completing historic mission to the Moon
items.summary: Four astronauts splashed down in the Pacific after a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans.

items.title: Irish cabinet meeting to be held to discuss ongoing fuel protests
items.summary: Hundreds of petrol stations in the Republic of Ireland have run out of fuel as the protests and blockades continued for a fifth day.

items.title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
items.summary: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

items.title: Trump unveils giant gold-accented victory arch design for US capital
items.summary: The new monument, if approved, would be taller than the US Capitol building and the Lincoln Memorial.

items.title: 'You are my god', Japan's PM tells British rock band Deep Purple
items.summary: Hard rock enthusiast Sanae Takaichi beamed as she met the rockers in her Tokyo office.

items.title: India refuses to criminalise marital rape. This new series shines a light on it
items.summary: Chiraiya, which has drawn an audience of millions, has prompted conversations on social media around consent and misogyny.

items.title: The prophet and the mysterious death of Charmain Speirs
items.summary: A BBC Disclosure investigation has uncovered significant questions about what happened at the hotel where Charmain died.

items.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
items.summary: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

items.title: Iran war lands 'triple blow' to flood-ravaged Sri Lankans
items.summary: The Iran war could not have come at a worse time for Sri Lanka, reeling from devastating floods and an economic crisis.

items.summary: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.
